Module com.example.demo2
Package com.example.demo2.model

Class SudokuBoard

java.lang.Object
com.example.demo2.model.SudokuBoard
public class SudokuBoard extends Object
Logic model for a dynamic 6x6 Sudoku game.

This class contains no view, controller, graphical interface, keyboard listeners, buttons, or Swing/JavaFX components. It only manages the internal Sudoku data and its validation rules.

The board structure is as follows:

  • 6 rows
  • 6 columns
  • 6 blocks of 2 rows × 3 columns
  • Valid numbers: 1 through 6

Game data is stored in HashMap and HashSet structures:

  • board: map "row,column" → current number
  • solution: map "row,column" → solution number
  • fixedCells: set of keys for non-editable cells

An empty cell simply does not exist in the board map, eliminating the need for a sentinel value of 0.

    Creates a new 6x6 Sudoku model and generates a new game.

  • Constructor Details

    • SudokuBoard

      public SudokuBoard()
      Creates a new 6x6 Sudoku model and generates a new game.

  • Method Details

    • generateNewGame

      public void generateNewGame()
      Generates a new Sudoku game.

      Clears all structures, generates a complete valid solution using backtracking, and places exactly two visible numbers in each 2×3 block.

    • canPlaceInMap

      public boolean canPlaceInMap(Map<String,Integer> map, int row, int column, int number)
      Checks whether a number can be placed in a cell of a given map.

      Validates the three Sudoku rules:

      • The number must not already exist in the same row.
      • The number must not already exist in the same column.
      • The number must not already exist in the same 2×3 block.
      Parameters:
      map - the map where the number will be checked
      row - row where the number will be placed
      column - column where the number will be placed
      number - number to validate
      Returns:
      true if the number can be placed

    • verifyMove

      public boolean verifyMove(int row, int column, int number)
      Checks whether the user can place a number in a specific cell.

      The current cell value is temporarily removed from the map before validation, allowing an already-entered value to be replaced.

      Parameters:
      row - row where the user wants to place the number
      column - column where the user wants to place the number
      number - number the user wants to place
      Returns:
      true if the move is valid

    • placeNumber

      public boolean placeNumber(int row, int column, int number)
      Places a number on the board if the move is valid.

      Internally calls verifyMove(int, int, int). If the move is valid, the number is added to the board map.

      Parameters:
      row - row where the number will be placed
      column - column where the number will be placed
      number - number to place
      Returns:
      true if the number was placed

    • clearCell

      public boolean clearCell(int row, int column)
      Clears a non-fixed cell by removing its entry from the board map.
      Parameters:
      row - row of the cell to clear
      column - column of the cell to clear
      Returns:
      true if the cell was cleared

    • isComplete

      public boolean isComplete()
      Checks whether the board is complete.

      The board is complete when the board map contains exactly 36 entries (6×6).

      Returns:
      true if all cells have a value

    • isBoardValid

      public boolean isBoardValid()
      Checks whether the current board state is valid according to Sudoku rules.

      Each value is temporarily removed from the map before validation to prevent a cell from detecting itself as a duplicate.

      Returns:
      true if the board is valid

    • isGameWon

      public boolean isGameWon()
      Checks whether the game has been won (board complete and valid).
      Returns:
      true if the game was won

    • getValue

      public int getValue(int row, int column)
      Returns the current value of a cell, or 0 if empty.
      Parameters:
      row - row of the cell
      column - column of the cell
      Returns:
      stored value, 0 if empty, -1 if position is invalid

    • getSolutionValue

      public int getSolutionValue(int row, int column)
      Returns the solution value at a specific cell.
      Parameters:
      row - row of the cell
      column - column of the cell
      Returns:
      solution value, or -1 if the position is invalid

    • isFixedCell

      public boolean isFixedCell(int row, int column)
      Checks whether a cell is fixed (not editable by the user).
      Parameters:
      row - row of the cell
      column - column of the cell
      Returns:
      true if the cell is fixed

    • isValidPosition

      public boolean isValidPosition(int row, int column)
      Checks whether a position is valid within the board.
      Parameters:
      row - row to validate
      column - column to validate
      Returns:
      true if the position is within the board

    • isValidNumber

      public boolean isValidNumber(int number)
      Checks whether a number is valid for a 6x6 Sudoku.
      Parameters:
      number - number to validate
      Returns:
      true if the number is between 1 and 6

    • getBoard

      public Map<String,Integer> getBoard()
      Returns a copy of the current board map.
      Returns:
      copy of the board as a Map

    • getFixedCells

      public Set<String> getFixedCells()
      Returns a copy of the fixed cells set.
      Returns:
      copy of the fixed keys set as a Set

    • showBoard

      public void showBoard()
      Prints the current state of the board to the console. Useful for debugging during development.
